macro_events.event_summary: AI-agent traffic to US retailer websites jumped 393% YoY in Q1 2026, with agentic shoppers spending more per session than human shoppers.
primary_drivers: Agentic AI shifts e-commerce discovery away from human browsing toward API/agent-mediated purchases
Retailers with strong product data feeds and agent-friendly infrastructure capture disproportionate share
Disintermediation risk for ad-supported search and traditional shopping UX

beneficiaries.rationale: Shopify has publicly rolled out agent-commerce infrastructure (Shop Pay, Catalog API, MCP integrations with OpenAI/Perplexity). Higher agentic checkout volume flows directly through merchants on its platform and takes rate.
Amazon's Rufus agent and structured product catalog make it a primary destination/endpoint for third-party shopping agents; larger basket sizes from agent shoppers directly lift GMV and ad revenue.
Walmart launched Sparky agent and partnered with OpenAI for agentic checkout; domestic retailer with structured inventory feeds directly benefits from higher agentic conversion.
Peer e-commerce platform read-through; MercadoLibre has been building agent-compatible APIs and would rally on the thesis that agent traffic expands TAM for platform commerce.
Global-E provides cross-border checkout infrastructure increasingly consumed by agents that route purchases internationally; benefits from any surge in programmatic checkout volume.
Klarna has partnered with OpenAI on agentic checkout and BNPL-at-agent-layer; higher agentic retail traffic drives payment volume through its rails.

losers.rationale: Agent-mediated shopping bypasses Google Search and Shopping ads — agents query product APIs directly. A 393% jump in agent traffic implies meaningful share shift away from the ad-funded discovery funnel that drives ~$60B+ of Google's retail ad revenue.
Similar disintermediation risk for Instagram/Facebook shopping ads; if agents complete purchases without social discovery, ad-impression value to retail advertisers declines.
Pinterest monetizes human visual discovery before purchase; agent shoppers skip the inspiration funnel entirely, eroding the shopping-ad narrative Pinterest has been selling.
Trade Desk's open-internet ad thesis weakens if an increasing share of retail transactions occur via agent APIs that don't pass through programmatic display ad auctions.

headlines: AI Traffic to US Retailers Jumps 393% in Q1 as Agentic Shoppers Outspend Humans
